preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Analytics API uses three routes: GET /analytics/dashboard, GET /analytics/metrics/:metricType, GET /analytics/reports/summary","detail":"Three focused endpoints keep concerns separated: dashboard gives an at-a-glance overview, metrics/:metricType allows drilling into specific metric categories (orders, users, revenue), and reports/summary provides a time-bounded aggregate report. This avoids a single monolithic endpoint while keeping the API surface small enough to implement incrementally.","tags":["api-design"],"scope":"src/routes/analytics.routes.ts","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KNBNC64C4C00S2S225T5PCAN","timestamp":"2026-04-04T07:12:33.420Z"}
{"entry_type":"decision","summary":"Analytics models use composable interfaces: TimeSeriesPoint, MetricSummary, DashboardData, and AnalyticsQuery for request params","detail":"Composable types: TimeSeriesPoint is the atomic unit (timestamp + value + optional label), MetricSummary aggregates a single metric (current, previous, change), DashboardData composes multiple MetricSummary instances plus time series. AnalyticsQuery standardizes request params (dateRange, granularity, metricType). This lets the service build responses incrementally and allows the dashboard to reuse the same MetricSummary type that individual metric endpoints return.","tags":["data-model"],"scope":"src/models/analytics.ts","agent_id":"agent-1","id":"01KNBND0F4Y1NWYYJ0QGG0BYH3","timestamp":"2026-04-04T07:13:00.388Z"}
